input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [TestMethod("display name")] and
[TestMethodAttribute("display name")] to set custom display names on tests.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these constructors no longer work because of
CallerInfo parameter changes. How should I update them?

data.content:

[TestMethodAttribute("display name")] to set custom display names on tests.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these constructors no longer work because of
CallerInfo parameter changes. How should I update them?

<current_datetime>2026-05-01T03:06:20.642Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [TestMethod("display name")] and
[TestMethodAttribute("display name")] to set custom display names on tests.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these constructors no longer work because of
CallerInfo parameter changes. How should I update them?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [TestMethod("display name")] and
[TestMethodAttribute("display name")] to set custom display names on tests.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these constructors no longer work because of
CallerInfo parameter changes. How should I update them?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about updating MSTest test methods after upgrading from v3 to v4. Let me look at their test files to understand the current code.

data.content:
In MSTest v4, the `TestMethodAttribute` constructor's first parameter became a `[CallerMemberName]` parameter, so passing a string there no longer sets the display name. Use the `DisplayName` named property instead:

```
[TestMethod("text")]  →  [TestMethod(DisplayName = "text")]
```

Let me fix your files:
data.reasoningText:
In MSTest v4, the `TestMethodAttribute` constructor changed. The first positional parameter now uses `[CallerMemberName]`, so passing a string to it no longer sets the display name — it sets the caller member name instead. The fix is to use the `DisplayName` property instead of the constructor parameter.

So `[TestMethod("display name")]` should become `[TestMethod(DisplayName = "display name")]`.

Let me also update the MSTest package version to v4.

data.content:
All 3 tests pass with MSTest v4. Here's what changed:

| Before (v3) | After (v4) |
|---|---|
| `[TestMethod("display name")]` | `[TestMethod(DisplayName = "display name")]` |
| `[TestMethodAttribute("display name")]` | `[TestMethod(DisplayName = "display name")]` |

**Why:** In MSTest v4 the `TestMethodAttribute` constructor's first positional parameter gained `[CallerMemberName]`, so passing a string there now overrides the caller info instead of setting the display name. Using the explicit `DisplayName` named property is the correct approach.

The package was also bumped from `3.8.0` → `4.2.2`.
